methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $318M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Svante is a Series E climate and energy company that has raised $318M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Lanzatech, ticker LNZA) operating in the Climate & Energy sector, including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

The stage-progression method is applicable and forms the sole quantitative anchor for this analysis. Using total capital raised of $318M as a baseline valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for Series E progression to the next stage, this method implies a valuation of $445M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 19 same-sector companies exist, no subset of three or more peers met the narrow filters of same-stage valuation, adjacent-stage proximity, or similar valuation tier simultaneously, making median-based triangulation unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $378M (low) to $512M (high), centered on $445M. This range reflects typical Series E advancement dynamics but carries material uncertainty stemming from the inability to validate against a robust peer cohort. Key risks to this valuation include the nascency of comparable exit data in frontier climate tech and volatility in public market multiples for the thin set of relevant public comparables. Conviction in any specific point within this range requires additional diligence on technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market timing relative to the peer set.
